purpose: single fixture that binds the pull handoff, push protocol, and test topology

pull_handoff.exporter: scans the merge base and coverage evidence
pull_handoff.importer: persists the base package as immutable provenance
pull_handoff.push_preflight: binds the persisted pull base to the live remote identity and a short-lived push session
pull_handoff.push_snapshot_hashes: lists the live remote comparison set for planning only
pull_handoff.push_plan_dry_run: uploads the canonical dry-run plan as eligibility evidence only
pull_handoff.push_batch_apply: applies mutation batches only after fresh live revalidation before every batch and again at the storage boundary
pull_handoff.push_journal: inspects durable claim, lease, and fencing evidence without granting write authority
pull_handoff.push_recover: inspects first, then finishes, rolls back, or blocks only when journal evidence plus fresh live hashes prove the action

production_shape.remote_snapshot_hash_listing: a cursorable live remote hash listing used only for planning
production_shape.dry_run_plan_upload: a canonical plan upload that yields a receipt but never a lock
production_shape.mutation_batch_apply: a batched write path that revalidates live state before every batch and at the storage boundary
production_shape.journal_inspect: a read-only evidence read that resolves ambiguity without authorizing a write
production_shape.recovery: inspect-first recovery that mutates only when fresh live hashes and journal evidence prove the action

proof: remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity at different times
browser-visible inspection goes through the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress
remote-base and remote-changed are the same remote identity at different times
browser-visible inspection goes through the sandbox-provided 8080 ingress

proofs.auth: push-auth-headers.json keeps read-only inspection on the existing HMAC family and requires push session, idempotency, and canonical push signature for dry-run, apply, and mutating recovery
proofs.session_journal: push-session-journal-proof.json binds the minted push session to the journal claim, lease fence, and inspect-first recovery path
proofs.recovery_decision: push-recovery-decision.json keeps inspect read-only and requires fresh live hashes before finish, rollback, or auto
proofs.recovery_path: push-recovery-path.json classifies the ambiguous apply outcomes as old, new, blocked, or open

required_invariants: dry-run and apply are separate remote operations
remote snapshot hash listing is planning evidence, not write authority
apply must revalidate the live remote before every batch and at the storage boundary
journal inspection is read-only and never authorizes mutation by itself
recovery must begin with inspect before any mutating repair
authentication must be at least as strict as current Reprint HMAC usage
